steps.title: Identify the goal
steps.content: The student wants to specify the order in which different government entities were given authority to regulate airline safety. The correct answer must show a clear chronological sequence of transfers — not just name the agencies, but show who passed authority to whom and when.

steps.title: Eliminate wrong choices and confirm the answer
steps.content:
• Choice A — The CAA had the authority to regulate safety for US airlines from 1938 until 1958, at which point authority was transferred to the US Commerce Department by Harold P. Navarro.: Gets the direction of transfer backwards — authority went from the Commerce Department to the CAA, not the other way around. Also misrepresents Navarro's role. ✗
  • Choice B — The FAA, CAA, and the US Commerce Department all had the authority to regulate US airline safety, but they possessed this authority at different times.: Names all three agencies but never specifies the chronological order of transfers. ✗
  • Choice C — The FAA has regulated airline safety since it was established by the US Congress in 1958.: Only mentions when the FAA started; says nothing about the sequence from Commerce Department to CAA to FAA. ✗
  • Choice D — The authority to regulate US airline safety transferred from the US Commerce Department to the CAA in 1938, then from the CAA to the FAA in 1958.: Lays out the full chronological sequence of transfers with exact years — precisely what the goal requires. ✓
That is the correct answer.
